requirement.description: The gateway shall periodically transmit the vehicle speed to the instrument cluster based on the most recently received data from the engine control module.
requirement.python_test_setup:
import can
import pytest
import time

MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS = 0xC8
GW_SPEED_SCALE = 0.1

@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(bustype="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    msgs = []
    end_time = time.time() + duration_sec
    while time.time() < end_time:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.5)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_gw_speed_kmh(msg):
    raw = msg.data[0] | (msg.data[1] << 8)
    return raw * GW_SPEED_SCALE

test_cases.title: Verify GW transmits speed value of 0 km/h when ECM speed is 0 km/h
test_cases.precondition: Vehicle ignition ON; CAN bus active; ECM transmitting ECM_VehicleData; CAN logger connected.
test_cases.expected_result: GW_SpeedOut = 0 km/h transmitted periodically on CAN.

test_cases.title: Verify GW forwards low-speed value (10 km/h)
Verify mid-range speed forwarding (80 km/h)
Verify maximum allowed speed (250 km/h)
test_cases.expected_result: GW_SpeedOut = 250 km/h without truncation or overflow.

test_cases.title: Verify GW updates speed when ECM value changes dynamically
test_cases.expected_result: GW always transmits the most recently received ECM speed.
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_gw_speed_005_dynamic_update(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 3)
    assert msgs
    speeds = [decode_gw_speed_kmh(m) for m in msgs]
    assert speeds[-1] == max(speeds)

test_cases.title: Verify GW retains last valid speed when ECM value is stable
test_cases.expected_result: GW_SpeedOut remains constant at 60 km/h across periodic transmissions.
